Patch Set 16: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(4 inline comments)

Patch Set 16:

One other question that I had is what happens if the LMA contains an invalid FID?  How can we detect if the LMA is corrupted with a garbage SEQ value, and repair this?  Does it even matter, or is the chance of such a corrupted FID being hit again too rare to worry about?

Patch Set 16:

> Patch Set 16:
> One other question that I had is what happens if the LMA contains an invalid FID? How can we detect if the LMA is corrupted with a garbage SEQ value, and repair this? Does it even matter, or is the chance of such a corrupted FID being hit again too rare to worry about?

Hi Andreas,

There may be several FID copies for an given object, for example: FID in parent dirent, FID in the OI file, and FID in LMA. Each one may be corrupt. Sometimes we can detect the crash, like invalid SEQ or VER; but sometime we cannot, because the FID may be changed to another valid FID. In ZFS, such data crash may be detected (and may be fixed) by block data checksums. But for ldiskfs, we almost can do nothing.

So now, I think it is better to trust the FID in LMA always, since it is the unique FID copy after restored from file-level backup. It is unnecessary to worry about the rare case of data integrity issues which should be processed by lower RAID or checksums.

Patch Set 24: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(20 inline comments)

Hi Fan Yong, after the patch is updated, please try to add the following info into the commit message to see whether the two test scripts will be performed by autotest or not:

Test-Parameters: envdefinitions=BACKUP=XXX,MINCOUNT=XXX,MAXCOUNT=XXX,FACTOR=XXX \
                 testlist=sanity-scrub,scrub-performance

Please refer to TT-282, which was fixed by Chris on last Friday.

parents.subject: LU-957 scrub: trigger OI scrub if found bad OI entry

commit.subject: LU-957 scrub: Proc interfaces and tests for OI scrub
commit.message:
LU-957 scrub: Proc interfaces and tests for OI scrub

1) Control/trace OI scrub running.

2) Verify whether the OI scrub basic functions works or not.

3) Test OI scrub performance.

Signed-off-by: Fan Yong <yong.fan@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I22a5271801e9cdc02dcb413e9b5859c673fd9759
